questions.questionMd: In the URL `https://www.example.com/api/users?id=123`, what does the `?id=123` portion represent?
questions.explanationMd: The portion of a URL following the question mark (`?`) is the query string, which assigns values to specified parameters.

questions.questionMd: According to the lecture, what is the fundamental difference between a single-page application (SPA) and a multi-page application (MPA)?

options.text: An SPA is much slower than an MPA due to heavy JavaScript use.
An SPA updates portions of the page dynamically using JavaScript, while an MPA requires a full page reload for every interaction.
An SPA can only have one HTML file, and no server backend.
MPAs are highly secure, while SPAs cannot utilize HTTPS.

questions.explanationMd: SPAs load a single HTML page and dynamically update content as the user interacts, whereas MPAs navigate to completely new HTML pages from the server.

questions.questionMd: Which of the following HTTP headers is used to indicate the media type of the resource?
questions.explanationMd: The `Content-Type` header tells the client what the content type of the returned content actually is (e.g., text/html, application/json).

questions.questionMd: How is an HTML input element's current value typically retrieved in JavaScript?

options.text: By parsing the `innerHTML` of the element
By accessing the `value` property of the input element's DOM object.

questions.explanationMd: Form inputs expose a `.value` property on their DOM node representation that contains the user's input.

questions.questionMd: Which of the following is a primary advantage of storing data in JSON format when using `fetch()`?

options.text: JSON files compress perfectly without algorithms
JavaScript has built-in support to automatically convert a JSON string into an object...
JSON does not require a web server to be fetched
JSON inherently blocks Cross-Site Scripting attacks

questions.explanationMd: Since JSON syntax is derived from JavaScript object notation, native functions like `JSON.parse()` make it extremely easy to deserialize.

questions.questionMd: To sort an array of album objects by year in ascending order, which comparator function should be passed to `.sort()`?
questions.explanationMd: Array `.sort()` determines order based on whether the return value is negative, zero, or positive. `a.year - b.year` sorts numerically in ascending order.

questions.questionMd: What is the fundamental difference between a URL that points to a static file and a URL that represents a REST API endpoint?

options.text: A static file URL always downloads a file, API endpoints render in the browser.
A REST API endpoint causes the server to dynamically generate a response...

questions.explanationMd: REST APIs are applications running on a server that process requests and generate responses on-the-fly, as opposed to a web server simply serving a flat file off a disk.

questions.questionMd: Given the object `let album = { title: "Daydream", year: 1995 };`, how would you access the year of the album?
questions.explanationMd: In JavaScript, object properties are accessed using dot notation (e.g., `object.property`) or bracket notation.

questions.questionMd: What is the event fired when an input field's value changes and it loses focus?
questions.explanationMd: The `change` event is fired for `<input>`, `<select>`, and `<textarea>` elements when the user modifies the element's value and then blurs (loses focus) from it.

questions.questionMd: In a Promise chain like `fetch(url).then(a).then(b)`, what value does function `b` receive as its argument?
questions.explanationMd: In a Promise chain, the return value of a `.then()` callback is passed as the argument to the next `.then()` callback in the chain.

questions.questionMd: According to the lecture, a variable that belongs to a class instance is called a:
questions.explanationMd: In object-oriented programming, variables attached to class instances are referred to as properties or fields.

questions.questionMd: Which command is used to serve local files over HTTP so that `fetch()` can access them during development?
questions.explanationMd: Python 3 has a built-in HTTP server module (`http.server`) that easily spins up a local web server to serve static files.

questions.questionMd: What is the primary purpose of the `.preventDefault()` method on an event object?

options.text: To stop the browser from performing the default action associated with the event (like submitting a form).
To pause JavaScript execution until the user clicks
To prevent other elements from overlapping

questions.explanationMd: Calling `event.preventDefault()` tells the browser not to execute its default behavior for that event, such as navigating to a new page on form submission or link click.

questions.questionMd: The `fetch()` API returns a Promise that resolves to what kind of object?

options.text: A `Response` object representing the response to the request.

questions.explanationMd: The Promise returned from `fetch()` won't reject on HTTP error status even if the response is an HTTP 404 or 500. It resolves normally with a `Response` object.

questions.questionMd: Why is the `fetch()` API described as asynchronous in the context of web programming?

options.text: Because it runs only on multi-core processors
Because it blocks the UI thread until the network responds
It allows the browser to continue executing other code while waiting for the network response.
Because it forces the server to respond immediately

questions.explanationMd: Asynchronous operations operate independently of the main program flow, ensuring the user interface remains responsive while network requests process in the background.
